root_text.an6.36:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī.
root_text.an6.36:1.4: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti.
root_text.an6.36:1.5: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satthari agāravo viharati appatisso, dhamme agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghe agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāya na paripūrakārī so saṅghe vivādaṁ janeti, yo hoti vivādo bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an6.36:1.6: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā samanupasseyyātha. Tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa pahānāya vāyameyyātha.
root_text.an6.36:1.7: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā na samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavāya paṭipajjeyyātha.
root_text.an6.36:1.8: Evametassa pāpakassa vivādamūlassa pahānaṁ hoti. Evametassa pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavo hoti.
root_text.an6.36:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu makkhī hoti paḷāsī …pe…
root_text.an6.36:2.6: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī, so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti.
root_text.an6.36:2.7: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satthari agāravo viharati appatisso, dhamme … saṅghe agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāya na paripūrakārī, so saṅghe vivādaṁ janeti, yo hoti vivādo bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an6.36:2.8: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā samanupasseyyātha. Tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa pahānāya vāyameyyātha.
root_text.an6.36:2.9: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā na samanupasseyyātha. Tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavāya paṭipajjeyyātha.
root_text.an6.36:2.10: Evametassa pāpakassa vivādamūlassa pahānaṁ hoti. Evametassa pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavo hoti.

translation_text.an6.36:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these six roots of dispute.
translation_text.an6.36:1.3: Firstly, a mendicant is irritable and acrimonious.
translation_text.an6.36:1.4: Such a mendicant lacks respect and reverence for the Teacher, the teaching, and the Saṅgha, and they don’t fulfill the training.
translation_text.an6.36:1.5: They create a dispute in the Saṅgha, which is for the detriment and suffering of the people, against the people, for the harm, detriment, and suffering of gods and humans.
translation_text.an6.36:1.6: If you see such a root of dispute in yourselves or others, you should try to give up this bad thing.
translation_text.an6.36:1.7: If you don’t see it, you should practice so that it doesn’t come up in the future.
translation_text.an6.36:1.8: That’s how to give up this bad root of dispute, so it doesn’t come up in the future.
translation_text.an6.36:2.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is offensive and contemptuous …
translation_text.an6.36:2.4: with corrupt wishes and wrong view …
translation_text.an6.36:2.5: They’re attached to their own views, holding them tight, and refusing to let go.
translation_text.an6.36:2.6: Such a mendicant lacks respect and reverence for the Teacher, the teaching, and the Saṅgha, and they don’t fulfill the training.
translation_text.an6.36:2.7: They create a dispute in the Saṅgha, which is for the detriment and unhappiness of the people, against the people, for the harm, detriment, and suffering of gods and humans.
translation_text.an6.36:2.8: If you see such a root of dispute in yourselves or others, you should try to give up this bad thing.
translation_text.an6.36:2.9: If you don’t see it, you should practice so that it doesn’t come up in the future.
translation_text.an6.36:2.10: That’s how to give up this bad root of dispute, so it doesn’t come up in the future.
translation_text.an6.36:2.11: These are the six roots of dispute.”
